name: getConfluence
description:
ALWAYS USE THIS TOOL for all Confluence operations. Never infer Confluence page content from conversation history - always fetch it fresh with op='read'. Supports create, append, read, list, delete, move, and raw API operations. Images and files must be referenced via public http/https URLs - local file paths are not supported. Markdown input is automatically converted to Confluence storage format.
WRITE SAFETY: Only perform create or append when the full intended content is already available in the current prompt or tool-call context. Do not claim that a Confluence write succeeded unless this tool actually succeeded in the current run. If the write result is ambiguous or does not clearly identify the target page, verify it with a follow-up read, list, or api call before reporting success. If verification is not possible, report the result as unverified instead of successful.
MULTI-WORKER: Multiple agents may append to DIFFERENT pages in parallel safely. Writing to the SAME page from multiple agents simultaneously causes version conflicts - serialize writes to the same page (one agent at a time).

CREATE WITHOUT PAGE ID: The space key and parent page are pre-configured — you do NOT need to provide a pageId when creating a new page. To create a page, use op='create' with title and markdown only. To append to an existing page, use op='append' with title (the tool resolves the page by title automatically). Never refuse a Confluence write task just because no explicit page ID was given.

LIST PAGINATION: op='list' responses include has_more and next_cursor for cursor-based pagination.
policyHint: For any Confluence operation - reading, creating, updating pages, or listing spaces - use this tool immediately without asking for confirmation. For writes, only proceed when the full content is already available, and verify ambiguous write results before claiming success.

imageUrl.description: Must start with http/https. Will be uploaded as an attachment.

imageUrls.description: All must start with http/https. Will be uploaded as attachments.

fileUrl.description: Any http/https URL - uploaded as an attachment.

embed.description: create/append: images embed inline by default; attach: default false (upload only).

cursor.description: For op='list': cursor for next page — use next_cursor from the previous response.
